feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Second SAP Stammtisch in Hout Bay – Glühwein, Good People, and Great Conversations
entry.content:
Despite the weather, seven SAP professionals made it out, creating a relaxed and welcoming round of discussions. With a glass of Glühwein (yes, glass — not mug!) in hand, the group quickly found common ground. The crowd was a vibrant mix: company owners, freelancers, and project managers, each bringing their own perspective on SAP and the local tech scene.

While we touched on everyday topics — from the recent Cape Town fires to life in the local consulting market — a few SAP themes dominated the evening:

  • Cloud vs. On-Premise: A discussion on what truly delivers value to customers today. We looked at flexibility, cost-efficiency, and long-term ownership — especially in the South African context.

  • SAP Fioneer: Some were curious about SAP’s financial services spin-off. We talked about its strategy, why it was spun off, and what it might mean for SAP’s industry cloud direction.

  • SAP’s retrenchment program: A topic that’s impossible to ignore. We talked about how it affects talent, partner ecosystems, and project dynamics — not just globally, but also here on the ground.

To make planning easier for the next round, we agreed to set up a dedicated WhatsApp group. It’ll serve as a home base for SAP professionals in the Cape Town area who want to stay connected, share knowledge, and meet up informally.

The atmosphere? Casual. The drinks? Comforting. The conversations? Absolutely worthwhile.

If you’re in or around Cape Town and work with SAP — keep an eye out. The Stammtisch is growing, and we’d love to have you at the next one. Read more on the Redcup Code website around the Cape Town SAP Stammtisch.

entry.title: Announcing SAP Inside Track Hyderabad: Join Us on July 26, 2025!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce that SAP Inside Track Hyderabad is back and better than ever! Mark your calendars for July 26, 2025, as we bring together SAP enthusiasts, experts, and professionals for a day of insightful sessions, networking, and innovation. Registration is now open, and we invite you to secure your spot for this exciting event.

What is SAP Inside Track?   
SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, architects, and leaders come together to share their knowledge and insights on various SAP-related topics. This grassroots initiative, organized by members of the SAP Community, provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on the latest SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!

Event Highlights

  1. Expert-Led Sessions
    • Keynote Speeches: Hear from industry leaders and SAP experts about the latest advancements in SAP technologies.
    • Technical Deep Dives: Participate in sessions that cover a wide range of topics, from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Cloud Platform, and everything in between.
    • Hands-On Workshops: Engage in interactive workshops designed to provide practical experience with SAP tools and solutions.
  2. Networking Opportunities
    • Meet the Experts: Connect with SAP professionals, developers, and consultants from various industries.
    • Community Building: Build relationships with peers who share your passion for SAP and technology.

Why Attend?

  • Expand Your Knowledge: Gain insights into the latest SAP technologies and trends.
  • Enhance Your Skills: Participate in hands-on workshops and technical sessions.
  • Grow Your Network: Connect with like-minded professionals and industry experts.
  • Get Inspired: Discover new ideas and innovative solutions that can transform your business.

Registration Details

Registration for SAP Inside Track Hyderabad in Open! Don’t miss this opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP. 

Visit the Registration Page: SAP Inside Track 2025

Note: This is an in-person event only.

Event Details

  • Date: July 26, 2025
  • Time: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM
  • Venue: SAP Callidus Cloud, 6th floor, Building No. 12-B, Raheja Mindspace IT Park (Google Maps)
  • Contact: For any queries, please contact us at sapcommunityhyderabad@gmail.com

Speaker Registration: 

Speaker registrations are now open! If you're an expert in building AI applications on SAP BTP, developing innovative Business AI solutions, or working with embedded AI in SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA or SuccessFactors, we welcome your contribution. We also invite partners and customers to showcase their work.   Link to Submit Speaker Nomination 

Exclusive Sponsorship Opportunities: 

With over 7500 community members across locations in the past 12 months, we’re eager to continue growing. If you're interested in sponsoring the event, please comment below, and we’ll reach out to you. This year, we have exclusive sponsor bundles that offer opportunities to showcase your content and expertise across multiple locations, including Bangalore, Gurgaon, Hyderabad, and Pune. 

Join the Conversation

Stay connected and join the conversation on social media using the hashtag #sitHYD. Follow us on Twitter and LinkedIn, for the latest updates and announcements.

We look forward to seeing you at SAP Inside Track Hyderabad 2025! Let’s come together to learn, share, and innovate.

 

Thanks & Regards

SAP Community HYD

entry.title: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Hyderabad 2025, July 26 2025
entry.content:
Event Overview

Date: July 26, 2025
Location: Hyderabad, India
Venue: SAP Callidus Cloud, 6th floor, Building No. 12-B, Raheja Mindspace IT Park (Google Maps)

SAP Inside Track Hyderabad is a community-driven event that brings together SAP users, developers, and experts from various industries. The event aims to foster learning, networking, and collaboration through a series of insightful sessions, workshops, and discussions.

Call for Speakers

We are looking for dynamic speakers to present on a variety of topics related to SAP technologies and solutions, we want to hear from you!

Topics of Interest

  1. Business AI Showcases & Use Cases
  2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  3. Embedded AI across Solutions - SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors & Other SAP Products
  4. SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors & Other SAP Products

Why Speak at SAP Inside Track Hyderabad?

  • Share Your Expertise: Showcase your knowledge and insights to a diverse and engaged audience.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with other SAP professionals, thought leaders, and industry experts.
  • Enhance Your Profile: Gain recognition and visibility within the SAP community.
  • Contribute to the Community: Play a part in the growth and development of the SAP ecosystem.

 How to register

To submit your proposal, please visit this link and complete the online submission form. If you have any questions or need further information, please contact us at sapcommunityhyderabad@gmail.com.

Next Steps: Once you submit your proposal,
Step 1: Our expert team will go through your proposal and if it is shortlisted, we will contact your for more details.
Step 2: This will again be discussed internally with the experts and once shortlisted, we will inform you about the next steps – like presentation format, social media advertisement etc.,

 

Thanks & Regards

SAP Community Hyd

entry.title: Calling All Applicants for the 2025 Premium Hub CoE High School Summer Orientation!
entry.content:
Each year, SAP’s Premium Hub Center of Expertise (CoE) in North America hosts high school students to participate in a weeklong Summer Orientation. The 25-hour program provides students with the opportunity to learn about SAP technologies through different activities while receiving educational and career guidance from professionals in the field. SAP experts lead daily, interactive sessions that cover a variety of exciting topics, from coding and data analytics to SAP Cloud, IoT, and Mobile platforms. Over the course of the week, students use their technical skills and newly acquired knowledge to resolve real-world scenarios using SAP technologies. This year we are excited to host the orientation in-person at the Newtown Square campus (outside of Philadelphia). There, students will be able to explore the office, as well as participate in hands-on activities.   

Every year the Summer Orientation features a jam-packed schedule, from expert innovation sessions to career development guidance. The week starts off with a brief introduction of SAP along with a presentation on the various internship opportunities offered to college students. Students also have a career tip session, where they receive professional advice about their educational journey and beyond. Later in the week, students get to meet Balaji Gaddam, Head of Premium Hub CoE North America.

Students work on a primary project throughout the week that is always relevant to the latest in technology. The students’ work has evolved over the years, much like how SAP’s products have evolved as well! In previous years of the program, students have built projects using a wide variety of SAP technologies. At the end of the week, students will be able to showcase their project to their fellow peers and Premium Hub CoE team members.  

High school students interested in attending the 2025 Premium Hub CoE Summer Orientation should fill out the form below and update their most recent transcript. Space is limited for this competitive program and is geared towards rising Juniors and Seniors. Transcripts are required to be considered for the program so they must be uploaded to be considered. No resumes or cover letters are needed. Ideal candidates will have a strong interest in STEM and a desire to pursue further study and/or a career in a STEM-related field.  

Apply to the STEM Week here.  

Applications will be accepted until Friday, June 20. Students accepted to the program will be notified of the decision by Friday, June 27. The orientation will run in-person at the SAP Newtown Square Campus from July 21 to July 25.  The general hours are 10 am to 3 pm but early dismissal on Friday. Transportation will need to be arranged for the student as we do not provide this. Please note that this is not an internship, it is an unpaid program, and all costs associated with travel will need to be covered by the student. SAP will not cover any costs.

entry.title: SAP Business Suite Webcast
entry.content:
Cерія вебкастів: SAP Business Suite для різних ролей у вашій організації

Чудова можливість поглибити знання про SAP Business Suite через призму різних бізнес-ролей! SAP запускає унікальну серію спеціалізованих вебкастів, кожен з яких розкриває потенціал платформи для конкретних управлінських позицій.

📅 Розклад заходів (червень-липень 2025)

Час проведення: 11:00 CEST (альтернативно: 12:00 SGT, 11:00 EDT)

Тиждень 1: Стратегічне управління та фінанси

  • 24 червня - CIO & CxO Track: "Stay agile and resilient in uncertain times with SAP Business Suite" Як залишатися гнучкими та стійкими в умовах невизначеності

  • 25 червня - CFO Track: "Finance-led ERP offerings in SAP Business Suite"
    ERP-рішення під керівництвом фінансової служби

  • 26 червня - COO Track: "Transform Supply Chain with SAP Business Suite" Трансформація ланцюга поставок

Тиждень 2: Закупівлі та HR

  • 8 липня - CPO Track: "Reimagine strategic and operation procurement with SAP Business Suite" Переосмислення стратегічних та операційних закупівель

  • 9 липня - CHRO Track: "Connecting People, data and processes with SAP Business Suite for HR" Об'єднання людей, даних та процесів в HR

🎯 Що вас чекає:

  • Персоналізований контент для кожної управлінської ролі
  • Живі Q&A сесії з експертами SAP
  • Практичні кейси та реальні сценарії впровадження
  • Інсайти щодо оптимізації бізнес-процесів через SAP Business Suite

💡 Чому це важливо:

У сучасному швидкозмінному бізнес-середовищі розуміння можливостей SAP Business Suite через призму вашої конкретної ролі може стати ключовим фактором успіху. Кожен вебкаст розкриває унікальні можливості платформи для вирішення специфічних викликів різних департаментів.

🔗 Реєструйтеся на вебкасти за посиланнями вище та поділіться своїм досвідом використання SAP Business Suite у коментарях - які виклики вашої ролі ви хотіли б вирішити за допомогою цих рішень?

 

#SAPUkraine30 #SAPCommunityKyiv #SAP

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content:
I was blogged forward by by @DanielMateo in this blog post. It’s always a pleasure to be back at the "Blog It Forward" challenge and be invited by a great promoter of knowledge sharing. I appreciate the invitation, Dani! 

I’ve shared my story here in my previous BIF years ago, but a lot has changed since then. Today, I’m still fully connected to the SAP ecosystem working remotely, leading projects, and training professionals, but with a lifestyle that’s much more balanced. I live in Recife, close to nature and, most importantly, close to what matters most: my wife and our two amazing teenagers. Working from home since 2020 has given me more focus and the space to live with greater purpose.

I go to the gym every day to lift weights. It’s been part of my routine for years. As I focused more on physical health, I also started paying closer attention to my spiritual life. 

Outside of SAP, what I love most is traveling with my wife and two kids. Our favorite destination is Porto de Galinhas. Every time we can, we go there. We wake up early, walk on the beach with our golden retriever Bolt, and enjoy a weekend barbecue.  

Fernando de Noronha 

When we don’t go to the beach, we drive to the countryside in Bonito/PE, is one of our favorite palece. Hiking, waterfalls, rappelling... and Bolt always comes with us. He loves swimming in rivers and lakes near to the many local waterfalls. After all that, he usually sleeps for over a full day to recover.

 One fun thing I’ve discovered recently is hiking with dogs. Yes, we go in groups, each person bringing their dog. It’s a fun mess ... a good one! Occasionally, Bolt gets into trouble with other dogs. He’s usually sweet, but not always 🙄.

About my activity in SAP world, I recently shared my story in the SAP Community Member Spotlight, I continue to share content on my social media channels (Youtube, Linkedin and Instagram), while leading SAP projects and actively supporting SAP Community events. My journey in the SAP Community goes beyond technology. It’s a place where I found purpose, friends, and amazing opportunities.

Thanks for reading this far. See you online, at the next SAP event, or maybe even on a trail, in nature, hiking with Bolt.

Now, answering Dani’s question:

With SAP's continuous push toward cloud-based solutions, how do you see the role of traditional SAP consultants evolving over the next 3–5 years?

The traditional SAP consultant must evolve and no longer just do system configurators. But to be business process designers. Those who understand integration, see the business value, and can explain it clearly will stand out. At the end of the day, it's all about turning complexity into real results for clients.

To continue the Blog It Forward, I nominate @RobertaFernande, @michellemachado and @SimoneAzevedo and leave them this question:

With S/4HANA, RISE with SAP, and BTP evolving rapidly, what do you think is essential for SAP clients to stay ahead in technology and digital transformation in the coming years?

entry.title: A Journey of Learning and Sharing – My Experience Teaching SAP at Universidade Portucalense
entry.content:
As this academic year closes in Portugal, I reflect on a significant chapter I had the privilege to be part of.

For many years, SAP and ERP systems have been central to my professional journey — complex, fascinating platforms supporting the heartbeat of countless organizations worldwide. This year, I took a step beyond my daily projects and brought this passion into the classroom.

At Universidade Portucalense in Porto, I was honored to teach the course Sistemas Integrados, part of the Information Systems for Management curriculum. Twenty students embarked with me on a semester-long exploration into the world of integrated enterprise systems.

What made this experience so enriching was not only the content, but also witnessing young students taking their very first steps into the SAP ecosystem. Through hands-on exercises on a live SAP S/4HANA system, they navigated business processes, created master data, posted financial transactions, and explored real-time analytics, both through the traditional SAP GUI and the modern Fiori interfaces.

I could see curiosity turning into understanding, and theoretical concepts transforming into practical skills. It’s always inspiring to watch that moment when the pieces start to come together — when the student realizes how companies are interconnected through these systems, and how technology drives daily business decisions.

But this journey also reminded me of something deeper: bridging the gap between academia and the business world is essential. Giving students early exposure to the tools and challenges that companies face daily is one of the best ways to prepare them for tomorrow’s job market demands.

Of course, none of this would have been possible without the support of Makevalue, which allowed me to teach these classes during my professional schedule. I’m deeply grateful for this opportunity to share my knowledge and, in return, to be energized by the enthusiasm and fresh perspectives that only students can bring.

Finally, a heartfelt thank you to Universidade Portucalense—an institution that values academic rigor and fosters strong connections with the business community, providing students with practical experiences that will serve them well throughout their careers.

I was a student at Universidade Portucalense, both for my undergraduate and master's degrees.

Teaching is always a privilege. And when it aligns with what drives you professionally, it becomes a truly special experience.

#SAPCommunity

entry.title: Innovating Banking Connectivity with SAP in Portugal – Join Us at SAP Inside Track Porto 2025
entry.content:
Innovating Banking Connectivity with SAP in Portugal – Join Us at SAP Inside Track Porto

We are excited to invite you to a joint session by Makevalue and SIBS at SAP Inside Track Porto. In this session, we will present a real-life case of innovation in banking connectivity using SAP technologies in Portugal.

Over the past few months, the Makevalue team has successfully implemented a suite of banking APIs, creating a modern and fully integrated solution within SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA:

Collections: APIs from SIBS were integrated, enabling seamless use of MB Way and Multibanco References within the SAP environment

Payments: The solution also includes real-time integration with Millennium BCP’s payment APIs, allowing end-to-end automation from SAP to bank systems

From Challenge to Innovation: A 6-Month Integration Journey

This project was delivered over six months and involved multidisciplinary finance, IT, and banking teams. It wasn’t just about technical integration but designing a resilient, scalable process to support day-to-day operations.

The implementation included standard payment and collection flows and advanced financial services such as Risco Sacado (Supplier Confirming), enhancing the organization’s working capital management and financial automation.

What to Expect at the Session

The session will feature project leaders and stakeholders who will share:

  • The journey from concept to go-live

  • Key technical and functional challenges

  • Lessons learned and best practices

  • A live demonstration of the integrated process running in SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA

Why You Should Attend

If you're working with SAP and looking to automate, simplify, and modernize banking communication in Portugal, this session will be of special value. You’ll see how SAP BTP and open banking APIs can work together to reduce manual work, increase control, and deliver strategic benefits to your finance department.

Join us at SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 on 27 September!

SAP Inside Track , #sitPOR , SAP Community

entry.title: Ювілей SAP Ukraine
entry.content:
🎉SAP Україна 30 років!

У 2025 році світовий лідер у сфері корпоративних додатків і генеративного ШІ SAP відзначає три десятиліття роботи на українському ринку. З 1995 року компанія є надійним технологічним партнером для бізнесу та держави, допомагаючи впроваджувати інновації та сучасні ІТ-рішення.

Лідерство на ринку. Згідно з рейтингами Forbes Україна, SAP — технологічна платформа вибору для українського бізнесу:

  • 7 з 10 провідних приватних компаній
  • 14 з 20 найкращих роботодавців країни
  • Більшість ключових інвесторів обирають SAP

Розвиток і локалізація. Від реєстрації представництва у 1995 році до повної локалізації — SAP адаптувала ключові продукти для українського ринку. Українська мова офіційно додана до стандартного інтерфейсу рішень. Хмарне портфоліо охоплює понад 100 рішень для 26 галузей та 12 напрямків діяльності. З 2022 року SAP Ukraine — резидент Дія.City.

Потужна екосистема.

  • 65 фахівців у локальному офісі
  • 18 партнерських компаній
  • Співпраця з AHK Ukraine, ЄБА, Lviv IT Cluster
  • Університетський Альянс із 13 ЗВО

Підтримка України. За 2022-2024 роки SAP надала українським клієнтам понад 65 млн євро допомоги у вигляді безкоштовних послуг. Компанія активно підтримує цифровізацію Міноборони, критичну інфраструктуру та медичні закупівлі. Українські експортери отримали безкоштовний доступ до SAP Business Network.

Соціальна відповідальність. SAP підтримує освітні та благодійні ініціативи: Social Impact Award Ukraine, центр "Superhumans", фонд "Діти Героїв", "UAnimals", програму "Дівчата STEM" та багато інших проєктів.

Загалом від початку повномасштабного вторгнення SAP виділила понад 4,5 млн євро на благодійність.

"30 років для SAP в Україні — це історія незламної підтримки, інновацій і зростання. Разом із нашими клієнтами, партнерами та командою ми не тільки трансформуємо бізнеси, а й активно сприяємо розвитку і процвітанню країни. Наше зобов’язання — продовжувати сприяти цифровізації української економіки, а також підтримувати суспільство на шляху до відновлення та процвітаючого майбутнього." — Ганна Мікулицька, керівна директорка SAP Ukraine.

Також ми маємо свою власну веб-сторінку, присвячену активності SAP у країні: SAP Ukraine!

#SAPUkraine30 #DigitalTransformation #SAPCommunityKyiv #SAP

entry.title: Hello SAP Community!
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I'm currently pursuing a Master's in Management of Technology (MOT) at Tokyo University of Science. My graduation research explores how the advancement of AI is reshaping SAP consulting — from automating routine tasks to expanding consultants’ roles into strategic, data-driven, and advisory functions.

To gain global perspectives from SAP professionals, I've created a brief survey. Your responses will help build a clearer picture of how SAP roles and operations may look in 2030.

📌 Please take a few minutes to share your views:
🔗 How will your SAP operations change in 2030?

Thank you so much for your time and valuable input!

entry.title: Be Part of the Action - Join SAP Inside Track Hyderabad This July
entry.content:
To All Members of the SAP Ecosystem,

We’re excited to share some fantastic news, SAP Inside Track Hyderabad is making a grand return on July 26, 2025! Whether you're a seasoned SAP professional or just beginning your journey, this is your chance to connect, collaborate, and grow with fellow enthusiasts and experts.

Join us for an inspiring day filled with knowledge-sharing sessions, hands-on insights, and valuable networking opportunities. We can’t wait to welcome you to a day dedicated to innovation and community spirit.

Event Snapshot:

Registrations Are Now Open!

Reserve your spot today and be part of this vibrant community event: [Click here to register]

Note: This is a fully in-person event, we’re excited to meet and engage with you face-to-face.

Got questions or interested in sharing your expertise through a session? Reach out to us anytime at sapcommunityhyderabad@gmail.com. We’d love to hear from you!

Let’s come together to learn, share, and celebrate the spirit of the SAP community. See you at SIT Hyderabad 2025!

For more information, please refer to the blog shared by @Ruthvik_Chowdary1  and Blog by @poonam_goje0382

entry.title: SAP facts
entry.content:
Привіт, SAP Community Kyiv!

Як ми вже згадували раніше, SAP Україна відзначає своє 30-річчя, тому сьогодні хочемо поділитися з вами кількома цікавими фактами про SAP, які демонструють справжній масштаб впливу компанії. Деякі з них можуть здивувати навіть досвідчених SAP-івців🤫

SAP буквально керує світовою економікою

  • Чи знали ви, що клієнти SAP представляють аж 84% світової економіки? Це не просто цифра – це свідчення того, наскільки глибоко програмне забезпечення SAP інтегроване в глобальні бізнес-процеси.
  • Ще більш вражаюче: 98 зі 100 найбільших компаній світу використовують рішення SAP. А серед компаній зі списку S&P 500 (це список 500 найбільших американських компаній за вартістю) цей показник становить 98%! Фактично, майже кожна велика компанія, яку ви знаєте, є клієнтом SAP.

SAP домінує в кожній галузі

🚗10 з 10 найбільших автомобільних компаній працюють з SAP.

🏦10 з 10 найбільших банків світу довіряють SAP.

🍔10 з 10 найбільших компаній харчової промисловості використовують SAP.

💻10 з 10 найбільших виробників технологічного обладнання – клієнти SAP.

🎬9 з 10 найбільших медіа та розважальних компаній обрали SAP.

💾9 з 10 найбільших компаній у сфері програмного забезпечення також використовують SAP (іронічно, чи не так?).

SAP любить не лише гігантів

  • SAP – це не лише для великих корпорацій: 40% компаній середнього бізнесу теж обрали SAP.
  • 6 з 10 найбільших компаній середнього ринку працюють з SAP.

Якщо подумати, це означає, що:

  • Майже кожен продукт, який ви купуєте, був вироблений, розповсюджений або проданий за допомогою систем SAP.
  • За ваші банківські операції, скоріше за все, відповідає SAP.
  • Ваша їжа, одяг, транспорт, електроніка – все це вже десь пройшло крізь «магію САП».

А яка статистика про SAP здивувала вас найбільше? Діліться своїми думками в коментарях👊🏻

Ці та ще більше цікавих фактів тут.

#SAPUkraine30 #SAPCommunityKyiv #SAPFacts #SAPCommunity# #SAP

entry.title: SAP Community Team Spotlight: Moshe Naveh - June 2025 Edition
entry.content:
You might have seen our iterations of recognizing active authors (one / two / three / four / five / six)  in SAP Community. We also want to introduce the team behind the SAP Community platform. This month we want to introduce @moshenaveh to you which I interviewed recently. Read more about how he got started and what his day to day looks like behind the scenes of the SAP Community.

 

Interview with Moshe

Santos, Marco (MS): How did you first get involved with the SAP Community? What was your starting point?

Moshe Naveh: I started back in 2009 when I was hired to be part of the Community team, back then it was still called SDN. My main responsibility at the time was supporting wiki users and editors. That was my first major project, and it gave me a great opportunity to get to know the community deeply. I also supported various operational and engagement-related tasks, so from the beginning, I was fully immersed in the day-to-day of the community and its contributors.

MS: That sounds great, now that we talked about the past let’s switch to the present: can you tell us what your current role on the SAP Community team is, and what a typical day looks like for you?

Moshe Naveh: I’m part of the SAP Community team, and my role is very diverse. A big part of my work is supporting community members across multiple channels. Whether it’s through the Community inbox, internal systems, or online discussions. I help identify different types of needs, like content, training, or even platform-related issues, and I work to address those in a way that benefits our users and stakeholders. 

My day usually starts with checking in on those support channels and then moves into daily moderation tasks. I also meet regularly with stakeholders and SAP Community collaborators, helping them understand how the community can support their goals. That includes coaching them on features, best practices, helping topic page editors with their contributions and how to increase engagement in the SAP Community.

I’m a people person, and I think that’s why I joined this team in the first place. It’s all about engagement and collaboration. On top of that, I’m constantly thinking of ways to improve the experience on the SAP Community and make it more enjoyable, whether that’s through challenges, engaging threads, or other interactive initiatives. One of the proudest moments for me was launching the "Blog It Forward" initiative.

Here are some examples:

What is your favorite dish or snack?

My favorite place is ... because...

Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday/celebration dish?

 MS: That sounds great. What’s one part of the whole community experience that you're especially proud of contributing to?

Moshe Naveh: It’s hard to choose just one thing, but what really stands out to me is the day-to-day support I provide to community members and encourage them to engage. A great example is a very interesting blog series by SAP Sapphire speakers. I genuinely enjoy interacting with them, whether it’s through the community inbox or other channels and listening to their needs. Helping them overcome challenges they face and seeing their positive feedback afterward really warms my heart. Those moments of connection and appreciation are what keep me going and remind me why this work matters so much.

MS: You have mentioned before that you have a very memorable moment or time that you remember very fondly in the community. Do you want to elaborate a little bit on that?

Moshe Naveh: Yes, absolutely. Back in 2012, I was out for a jog. Funny enough, that’s often when my creativity kicks in. I was thinking about new ways to engage people in the community, and that’s when the idea for Blog It Forward came to me. It was a chain of blog posts where one person would write about themselves using a set of suggested questions, include some personal photos, and then “forward” the challenge to others. It quickly grew into a global storytelling movement with hundreds of participants. Even today, people remember it as one of the most enjoyable initiatives we’ve had and I’m incredibly proud to have sparked that.

MS: Can you elaborate a little bit on how you stay in touch with community needs and feedback?

Moshe Naveh: Sure. There are multiple ways we stay connected with the community. We regularly monitor our discussion forums and content areas to see what’s being posted. We also hold stakeholder meetings and participate in community calls where people share their thoughts directly. Many members reach out to us via email or internal channels, but the best way to contact the team is through the community@sap.com inbox. That’s where we gather a lot of insights and feedback. It helps us understand what members need and how we can support them better.

MS: If there’s one thing that you would really love to see more of in the community, what would that be?

Moshe Naveh: I always enjoy seeing and meeting new people in the community. Every new member brings their own qualities, experiences, and contributions, and that enriches the entire community. So, I’d love to see more new members joining. Creating a welcoming and inclusive environment is really close to my heart—that’s something we focused on in the Blog It Forward initiative and in other engaging threads. It’s also why I recently shared a blog post on using alternative text to make community content more accessible and inclusive.

MS: Great to hear that. And now we're at the last question of the interview: is there anything else that you would like to share?

Moshe Naveh: I think it's often a challenge for people to take that first step and start engaging in the community. But once they do, everything becomes easier. So I encourage everyone to just say hi, explore the different ways of participating, share your knowledge, and grow together with others. It’s truly a journey we can all take together—and it’s one that’s definitely worth it. 

MS: This is a  great message to end the interview. Thank you so much for joining today and sharing your experiences with the community!

entry.title: Architecture Gone Wrong: Leveraging SAP Architecture Center for Better Outcomes
entry.content:
In the today growing software development, architecture plays a crucial role in determining the success and efficiency of a system. Poor architecture can lead to significant costs, performance issues, and maintenance headaches.

This blog explores the pitfalls of bad architecture and highlights the advantages of leveraging the SAP Architecture Center to ensure best practices and cost savings.

The Pitfalls of Poor Architecture

1. High Costs: Poorly designed architecture can lead to increased costs in several ways:

  • Maintenance: Systems with bad architecture require frequent fixes and updates, leading to higher maintenance costs.
  • Scalability: Inefficient architecture can struggle to scale, necessitating expensive overhauls or replacements.
  • Performance: Suboptimal architecture can result in slow performance, requiring additional resources to meet performance standards.

2. Performance Issues: Bad architecture can severely impact system performance:

  • Latency: Inefficient data flow and processing can lead to high latency, affecting user experience.
  • Reliability: Poor architecture can result in frequent downtimes and failures, reducing system reliability.
  • Resource Utilization: Inefficient use of resources can lead to higher operational costs and lower performance.

The SAP Architecture Center: A Solution

The SAP Architecture Center offers a comprehensive suite of tools, best practices, and reference architectures to help businesses design efficient and cost-effective systems

https://architecture.learning.sap.com 

Here are some key benefits:

1. Best Practices: The SAP Architecture Center provides access to industry best practices, ensuring that your architecture is designed to meet the highest standards. This includes guidelines for security, performance, and scalability.

2. Reference Architectures: Reference architectures are templates that outline the interactions between various services and components. They provide a standardized approach to designing systems, ensuring optimal configurations and seamless integration 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchCatalog/ 

3. Community-Driven Guidance: The SAP Architecture Center fosters a community of practice where architects and developers can share insights, collaborate, and learn from each other. This collaborative environment helps in adopting proven frameworks and innovative solutions

https://architecture.learning.sap.com/community/intro 

Advantages and Benefits

1. Cost Savings: By following SAP best practices and utilizing reference architectures, businesses can significantly reduce costs:

  • Reduced Maintenance: Well-designed architecture requires less frequent fixes and updates.
  • Efficient Resource Utilization: Optimal configurations ensure efficient use of resources, reducing operational costs.
  • Scalability: Scalable architecture minimizes the need for expensive overhauls.

2. Improved Performance: Leveraging the SAP Architecture Center can lead to better system performance:

  • Lower Latency: Efficient data flow and processing reduce latency.
  • Higher Reliability: Robust architecture ensures fewer downtimes and failures.
  • Optimal Resource Utilization: Efficient use of resources leads to better performance.

FinOps for Cost Saving

Financial Operations (FinOps) is a practice that combines financial management with cloud operations to optimize costs. Integrating FinOps with SAP best practices can further enhance cost savings:

  1. Cost Monitoring: FinOps tools help in monitoring cloud spending, identifying cost drivers, and optimizing resource usage.
  2. Budgeting and Forecasting: FinOps practices enable accurate budgeting and forecasting, ensuring that financial resources are allocated efficiently.
  3. Continuous Improvement: FinOps encourages continuous improvement by regularly reviewing and optimizing cloud spending, leading to sustained cost savings.

Conclusion

Poor architecture can lead to significant costs and performance issues. However, by leveraging the SAP Architecture Center and adopting FinOps practices, businesses can design efficient, cost-effective, and high-performing systems. The SAP Architecture Center provides best practices, reference architectures, and community-driven guidance to ensure that your architecture is built to the highest standards. Embrace these tools and practices to avoid the pitfalls of bad architecture and achieve optimal results.

entry.title: Come join us for the SAP TechEd Community Cluetrain 2025 edition
entry.content:
For me, personally, SAP TechEd 2008 in Berlin, is where the Community really began for me. 
While I also attended the 2007 TechEd, TechEd 2008 in Berlin is the conference that will always be on the top of my list over unforgettable conferences attended. 

TechEd Berlin 2008 is where I met many people I still consider friends today for the first time face-to-face, the Community Clubhouse was the go-to-place, and I got to experience being on the DemoJam stage with Dick Hirsch demoing the best community-led project ever (shameless self-promotion); ESME:

If you were there in 2008, you will perhaps also recognise many faces in this video from Dennis Howlett: 


Another highlight of TechEd 2008 for me, was the SAP TechEd Cluetrain that @craigcmehil arranged. 
Since the 2025 TechEd in Berlin is all about the SAP Community coming home for me, I thought why not also relaunch the Cluetrain this year?

In 2008 we were about 25 Community members and friends who got together, and took the train together from Frankfurt to Berlin to get the conversations started. 
@gregorw published this post, and @JimSpath shared this post, about the trip. 

This year we will meet at Frankfurt Main Hbf (Frankfurt Main Central station), and take the train departing Monday, November 3rd at 13:14 CET (1:14 pm) together: 

This time we will need to try to self-coordinate buying tickets individually so that we end up sitting in the same coach. 
To start I have already booked my ticket - you will find me in coach 4, seat 91. 
When you book your tickets, please make sure you book the ticket with seat reservation and pick a seat in coach 4:

I will be at the central station about an hour before the train departs. I will add a comment on this blog post as we get closer which café you can find me in. 

After arriving in Berlin, the idea is that we will all move on to attend a SAP Stammtisch together. 
Our Berlin group owner, @rofinke, is out on vacation right now, so time and place is still to be announced. 
@rofinke - FYI for when you are back, we will host a Stammtisch together on November 3rd 😁  (please check your private messages when you are back online)

Big shout out to @gregorw - as always, thank you for your support!

I will end this post with the same shoutout that Craig did in 2008:
Several hundred kilometers of conversation, community, collaboration and co-innovation! 
I hope to see many of you on the train 🙂
(if you book a ticket, let us know by adding a comment below)

  • SAP TechEd registration is now live, you can read more about it here.
  • DemoJam is back this year as well, check out this post from @cecihuergo 
  • Aaaand the Community Talks are back as well

#sapcommunityiscominghome 🥳

entry.title: Нові вебкасти
entry.content:
Серія вебкастів: SAP Трансформація та Хмарні Рішення

Можливість поглибити знання про SAP через призму сучасних технологій та стратегій цифрової трансформації: SAP представляє комплексну серію вебкастів, присвячених переходу до хмарних рішень, автоматизації процесів та максимізації потенціалу SAP екосистеми.

📅Розклад заходів (липень 2025) Всі вебінари вказані за київським часом

Тиждень 1: Стратегія переходу до хмари

  • 3 липня - SAP Business Suite: "From SAP Business Suite to SAP Cloud ERP: Realizing the Strategy" Час: 15:00 (45 хв) Реалізація стратегії переходу від ECC до хмарних рішень через автоматизацію та розумну аналітику
  • 8 липня - Transformation: "RISE with SAP Methodology with the Integrated Toolchain" Час: 15:00 (60 хв) Комплексний підхід до трансформації: процеси, додатки, люди та дані
  • 10 липня - SAP Business Suite: "Unlocking the Cloud: Accelerate Your ERP Transformation Journey" Час: 9:00 / 15:00 (45 хв) Реалізація стратегії SAP Business Suite в SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Тиждень 2: Управління ліцензіями та функціональність

  • 15 липня - SAP for Me: "License Consumption" Час: 15:00 (60 хв) Цифровий супутник для прозорого управління ліцензіями
  • 22 липня - SAP for Me: "Functional Usage" Час: 15:00 (60 хв) Аналіз функціональності та виявлення невикористаного потенціалу

Тиждень 3-4: Досвід користувачів та інтеграція

21-25 липня - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 Early Release Series сесії з експертами, присвячені інноваціям випуску 2508. Включають AI-функції та вдосконалення з Joule

  • 29 липня - SAP for Me: "Product Experience" Час: 15:00 (60 хв) Пілотний проєкт для покращення користувацького досвіду
  • 29 липня - SAP Integration Suite: "Monthly Update July" Час: 17:00 (60 хв) Оновлення платформи інтеграції як послуги (iPaaS)

🎯Що вас чекає:

  • Практичні демонстрації інструментів SAP LeanIX, Signavio, Cloud ALM та WalkMe
  • Живі Q&A сесії з продуктовими експертами
  • Ексклюзивні попередні перегляди майбутніх інновацій
  • Реальні кейси успішної трансформації клієнтів
  • Інсайти щодо штучного інтелекту та автоматизації процесів

💡Чому це важливо: Цифрова трансформація з SAP вимагає стратегічного підходу до міграції, оптимізації процесів та управління змінами. Ця серія вебкастів надає комплексне розуміння всіх аспектів переходу - від технічних деталей до бізнес-стратегії.

🔗Приєднуйтесь до вебкастів та діліться своїм досвідом цифрової трансформації - які виклики вашої організації ви хотіли б вирішити за допомогою SAP хмарних рішень?

#SAPUkraine30 #SAPCommunityKyiv #SAP #DigitalTransformation #CloudERP

entry.title: Heartiest congratulations to our newest SAP Champions in July 2025!
entry.content:
Please join the SAP Community team in welcoming 8 new SAP Champions to the family, and program, in July 2025. Happy to say the new additions bring a diverse mix of location, expertise, traits and charm!


Meet the class of July 2025!

Antonio de Ancos Cid, SpainMio Yasutake, Japan
Ewelina Siudzińska, PolandPedro Vieira, Portugal
Harish Vatsa, UAERaja Gupta, India
Jonys Arcanjo, BrazilRoberta Fernandes, Brazil

About the SAP Champions program

The SAP Champions program was established in 2019. The program provides the framework to allow participants to engage optimally across SAP Community & ecosystem. The program participants are a mix of partners, customers, independents, professors, and SAP employees.

Who are the SAP Champions?

In a nutshell, they are valued SAP Community members who inspire others to be like them. The SAP Champions are volunteers. They are top contributors, leaders, and organizers within the SAP ecosystem. They not only have a robust reputation in SAP Community but also help to drive software adoption and act as advocates in their own networks.

How can I connect with them?

Come and meet each of our champions in our SAP Community Leaders Finder.

Where do they live, and are there any in my region?

We now have 85 champions worldwide. In January of this year - we welcomed 10 new champions to the program. Since there are champions in each region, why not reach out to one near you and connect? They might even support you in your local community and help you to get better connected. It is possible to send a private message on SAP Community via the individual blog posts in our finder. In addition, you could connect with them on social media.

To find champions in your region: North America | Latin America | EMEA | APAC

What are SAP Champions doing in 2025?

Our champions are all individual people with different interests and expertise but the one thing they tend to have in common is the passion to proactively nurture a local community. They are an amazing group of proactive human beings who collectively love to: blog | connect with others |  continuously learn and share | contribute positively in any way possible.

Our focus is clear this year with SAP Certification Transformation being a priority. There has also recently been an announcement about: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers. Our champions are supporting with the key messaging around these types of topics.

We also continue to focus on SAP Community events. Any SAP Community member can become an event organizer, but many of our SAP Community Leaders (both champions and mentors) already actively organize, co-host, speak at events globally.

Some other highlights from the year so far:

  • Community member spotlight - Marssel Vilaça | Manual Robalinho
  • Blog it forward campaign contributions from: Marco Büscher | Marssel Vilaça | Sandra Solis | Lluis Salvador | Daniel Mateo - and many more to come throughout the year! Watch this space!

Who can become an SAP Champion?

Anyone who is an SAP Community member for a year or more could potentially aspire to be an SAP Champion. We are always looking for new members who are active in their local communities, people who like to organize or speak at events, or support other community members.
Find out what makes a great SAP Champion.
To nominate someone, please reach out to sapchampions@sap.com. 

@josh_bentley | @StephanieMarley | @kimmecca | @AnnikaLink | @David_Chaviano1 | @AAncos | @ewelina | @Harish_Vatsa65  | @Jonys_Arcanjo | @MioYasutake | @Pedro_Vieira | @Raja_Gupta1 | @RobertaFernande | @Sandra_Solis | @marcobuescher | @Marssel700 | @Sandra_Solis | @DanielMateo | @MRobalinho | @Lluis 

#SAPCertification | #SAPChampions

entry.title: Regional Leaders - SAP Champions Program
entry.content:
In July 2025, we see the introduction of Regional Leaders within the framework of the SAP Champions program in SAP Community. 

Business reasons:

  • Connecting SAP Champions more closely to one another, within regions - strengthening connections
  • Bridge the location gaps in the program, and time zone challenges
  • Extend the community team reach, outside of our time zones
  • Act as a catalyst to grow and scale the program in the future
  • Grow awareness of SAP Community and programs in different regions

What is a Regional Leader?

  • An existing SAP Champion who can communicate well with the program manager
  • A trusted SPOC for their region
  • Someone interested to help improve and grow the SAP Champions program
  • A catalyst person for attracting new talent to the program, especially in relation to diversity gaps (e.g. regions where we do not have any champions as of yet)

Meet Murali Shanmugham

 

Regional Leader APAC, SAP Champions Program

Global Business AI Strategy Advisor

Sydney, Australia

 

Meet Chandra Bhushan Singh 

 

Regional Leader APAC, SAP Champions Program

CSP

Gurgaon, India

 

* Note: in the pilot phase, we will focus on the APAC region - bridging the time zones to the community team (mainly based in EMEA, NA). This will help us to determine if the concept works, and can be rolled out globally.

For further information about the SAP Champions program - reach out to sapchampions@sap.com 

@Murali_Shanmugham | @ChandraBhushanSingh

entry.title: 🌟 Community Spotlight: Our Top Contributors June 2025 🌟
entry.content:
We are continuing our series of celebrating the SAP Community’s top contributors.
Huge THANK YOU from our side to our June 2025 top contributors 🎉

Top Accepted Solution Authors non-SAP employees:

Our community members who took the time to help others by answering questions, and who had the most answers accepted as solutions within their area of expertise. 

  • CRM and Customer Experience: @DonWilliams 
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @Chuma 
  • Financial Management: @Jeremy_Deo 
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @ArunSuresh3 
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @DonWilliams
  • Spend Management: @dhinesh_thirugnanam 
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @Chuma 
  • Technology: @DonWilliams 

Top Accepted Solution Authors SAP employees:

Our community members who took the time to help others by answering questions, and who had the most answers accepted as solutions within their area of expertise. 

  • CRM and Customer Experience: @Silvia_Ventura 
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @Edrilan_Berisha 
  • Financial Management: @HenrikeGrtecke 
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @Gopinath 
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @ManoelCosta 
  • Spend Management: @Prem_Tracz 
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @ManoelCosta 
  • Technology: @DoloresCorrea 


Top Kudoed Authors non-SAP employees:

Our blog post authors who published valuable content that other community members kudoed. 

  • SAP Community overall: @Marssel700 
  • CRM and Customer Experience: @pvsbprasad 
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @Amin_Omidy 
  • Financial Management: @Chuma 
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @StephanieBM01 
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @Ramnarayana_Valluri 
  • Spend Management: @Karthikeyan_C 
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @Ajit_Routray 
  • Technology: @Sandra_Rossi 

Top Kudoed Authors SAP employees:

Our blog post authors who published valuable content that other community members kudoed. 

  • SAP Community overall: @ChrisGiall 
  • CRM and Customer Experience: @GuillaumeLasnier 
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @Andrew_Ford 
  • Financial Management: @Nupur_Agrawal 
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @xavierlegarrec 
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @Laxmipathivarma 
  • Spend Management: @cherry_chen2 
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @Altan 
  • Technology: @ChrisGiall 

Top Kudo Givers non-SAP employees:

The community members who took the time to give kudos to other members.  

  • SAP Community overall: @zfiori 
  • CRM and Customer Experience: @dhruv_mehta 
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @maurojara3 
  • Financial Management: @PauloZeferino 
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @Dren 
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @Ali_ben 
  • Spend Management: @Shella 
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @SriLaxmiSirisha_Reddy 
  • Technology: @zfiori 

Top Kudo Givers SAP employees:

The community members who took the time to give kudos to other members.  

  • SAP Community overall: @Vitaliy-R 
  • CRM and Customer Experience: @larissarauzer 
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @LouisChen 
  • Financial Management: @Anita_Ahmadpour 
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @Sheryle 
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @samuel_bianchi 
  • Spend Management: @bruno_labedan 
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @Altan 
  • Technology: @CarlosDiniz 


Want to be in next month’s spotlight?

Keep doing what you’re doing: write quality blog posts that other community members kudo and share your knowledge by answering questions. Help each other. Engage with other community members in the comments and in our group discussions.

A shout-out to our community members who ask questions:

If you have asked a question, please remember to accept correct answers as solutions.

When you receive a helpful response, marking it as the Accepted Solution allows others with similar issues to find the best answer quickly. This feature also highlights the most useful responses for future reference.
By accepting answers as solutions, you also give your fellow community members who take the time to help you, their deserved recognition on our leaderboards.

entry.title: The CAPstone Project: A Journey from Component to Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

In this post, I'll take you through complete journey: of building React + Vite(Component) application on SAP’s ecosystem(Cloud).

Someone, who is React developer and curious about SAP's environment or a CAP expert just exploring modern frontend option, this blog will give them a practical and real-world solution with code snippets.

Let's dive how modern JavaScript development can thrive alongside enterprise SAP capabilities.

Open BAS 

Create a CAP Project

 

cds init project_name
npm install

 

Create a basic schema schema.cds

 

namespace UserManagement;

using { managed, cuid } from '@sap/cds/common';

entity Users : managed, cuid {
    name    : String(100) not null;
    email   : String(255) not null;
    company : String(100) not null;
}

 

Service in service.cds

 

using UserManagement from '../db/schema';

service UserService {
    entity Users as projection on UserManagement.Users;

    function getUser() returns array of {
        name    : String(100) not null;
        email   : String(255) not null;
        company : String(100) not null;
    }
}

 

Custom service service.js

 

const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const { message } = require('@sap/cds/lib/log/cds-error');

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function() {
    const { Users } = this.entities;

    // Before CREATE event - validate data
    this.before('CREATE', 'Users', async (req) => {
        const { name, email, company } = req.data;
        
        // Basic validation
        if (!name || !email || !company) {
            req.reject(400, 'All fields (name, email, company) are required');
        }
        
        // Email validation
        const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
        if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {
            req.reject(400, 'Invalid email format');
        }
        
        // Check for duplicate email
        const existingUser = await SELECT.one.from(Users).where({ email: email });
        if (existingUser) {
            req.reject(400, 'User with this email already exists');
        }
    });

    // After CREATE event - log the action
    this.after('CREATE', 'Users', async (data, req) => {
        console.log(`New user created: ${data.name} (${data.email})`);
    });

    this.on('getUser',async(req)=>{
        const data = await SELECT.from('Users');
        return {status:200,message:'Data Fetched',data}
        
    })
});

 

Add csv file for mock data

 

cds add data

 

Add the below mock data

 

ID,name,email,company,createdAt,modifiedAt
1,John Doe,john.doe@example.com,Tech Corp,2024-01-15T10:00:00Z,2024-01-15T10:00:00Z
2,Jane Smith,jane.smith@innovate.io,Innovate Solutions,2024-01-16T11:30:00Z,2024-01-16T11:30:00Z
3,Mike Johnson,mike.j@startup.com,StartupX,2024-01-17T09:15:00Z,2024-01-17T09:15:00Z

 

Now let’s Create React Project

 

cd app
npm create vite@latest .

 

As shown below

Install Lucide React for icon

 

npm install lucide-react

 

Install Tailwind CSS(optional)

Setup Proxy Server to avoid CORS issue

vite.config.js

 

import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

// https://vite.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react(),tailwindcss()],
  server:{
    proxy:{
      '/odata':{
        target:'http://localhost:42745',
        changeOrigin:true,
        rewrite: path=>path.replace('/^\/odata/','/odata'),
      }
    }
  }
})

 

Create the UI

App.jsx

 

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { Plus, Database, User, Mail, Building } from 'lucide-react';
import './app.css'

const App = () => {
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
    name: '',
    email: '',
    company: ''
  });
  const [users, setUsers] = useState([]);
  const [showTable, setShowTable] = useState(false);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);

  // Nord color palette
  const nordColors = {
    polarNight: ['#2e3440', '#3b4252', '#434c5e', '#4c566a'],
    snowStorm: ['#d8dee9', '#e5e9f0', '#eceff4'],
    frost: ['#8fbcbb', '#88c0d0', '#81a1c1', '#5e81ac'],
    aurora: ['#bf616a', '#d08770', '#ebcb8b', '#a3be8c', '#b48ead']
  };

  const handleInputChange = (e) => {
    setFormData({
      ...formData,
      [e.target.name]: e.target.value
    });
  };

  const handleSubmit = async () => {
    if (!formData.name || !formData.email || !formData.company) {
      alert('Please fill in all fields');
      return;
    }
    
    setLoading(true);
    
    try {
      // Simulate API call to CAP backend
      const response = await fetch('odata/v4/user/Users', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(formData)
      });
      
      if (response.ok) {
        // For demo purposes, add to local state
        const newUser = {
          id: Date.now(),
          ...formData,
          createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
        };
        setUsers(prev => [...prev, newUser]);
        setFormData({ name: '', email: '', company: '' });
        alert('User saved successfully!');
      }
    } catch (error) {
      // For demo, still add to local state
      const newUser = {
        id: Date.now(),
        ...formData,
        createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
      };
      setUsers(prev => [...prev, newUser]);
      setFormData({ name: '', email: '', company: '' });
      alert('User saved successfully! (Demo mode)');
    }
    
    setLoading(false);
  };

  const fetchUsers = async () => {
    setLoading(true);
    setShowTable(true);
    
    try {
      // Simulate API call to fetch users
      const response = await fetch('odata/v4/user/getUser');
      if (response.ok) {
        const data = await response.json();
        console.log('log on ui',data.value[0].data)
        setUsers(data.value[0].data);
      }
    } catch (error) {
      // Demo mode - use local state
      console.log('Using demo data');
    }
    
    setLoading(false);
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen" style={{ backgroundColor: nordColors.polarNight[0] }}>
      <div className="container mx-auto px-4 py-8">
        {/* Header */}
        <div className="text-center mb-8">
          <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold mb-2" style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[2] }}>
            User Management
          </h1>
          <p className="text-lg" style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[0] }}>
            Simple form with SAP BTP CAP backend
          </p>
        </div>

        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-8">
          {/* Form Section */}
          <div 
            className="p-6 rounded-lg shadow-xl"
            style={{ backgroundColor: nordColors.polarNight[1] }}
          >
            <h2 
              className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-6 flex items-center gap-2"
              style={{ color: nordColors.frost[1] }}
            >
              <Plus className="w-6 h-6" />
              Add New User
            </h2>
            
            <div className="space-y-4">
              <div>
                <label 
                  className="block text-sm font-medium mb-2 flex items-center gap-2"
                  style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[1] }}
                >
                  <User className="w-4 h-4" />
                  Name
                </label>
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="name"
                  value={formData.name}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  required
                  className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-md border focus:ring-2 focus:outline-none transition-all"
                  style={{ 
                    backgroundColor: nordColors.polarNight[2],
                    borderColor: nordColors.polarNight[3],
                    color: nordColors.snowStorm[2],
                    focusRingColor: nordColors.frost[2]
                  }}
                  placeholder="Enter full name"
                />
              </div>

              <div>
                <label 
                  className="block text-sm font-medium mb-2 flex items-center gap-2"
                  style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[1] }}
                >
                  <Mail className="w-4 h-4" />
                  Email
                </label>
                <input
                  type="email"
                  name="email"
                  value={formData.email}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  required
                  className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-md border focus:ring-2 focus:outline-none transition-all"
                  style={{ 
                    backgroundColor: nordColors.polarNight[2],
                    borderColor: nordColors.polarNight[3],
                    color: nordColors.snowStorm[2]
                  }}
                  placeholder="Enter email address"
                />
              </div>

              <div>
                <label 
                  className="block text-sm font-medium mb-2 flex items-center gap-2"
                  style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[1] }}
                >
                  <Building className="w-4 h-4" />
                  Company
                </label>
                <input
                  type="text"
                  name="company"
                  value={formData.company}
                  onChange={handleInputChange}
                  required
                  className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-md border focus:ring-2 focus:outline-none transition-all"
                  style={{ 
                    backgroundColor: nordColors.polarNight[2],
                    borderColor: nordColors.polarNight[3],
                    color: nordColors.snowStorm[2]
                  }}
                  placeholder="Enter company name"
                />
              </div>

              <button
                type="button"
                onClick={handleSubmit}
                disabled={loading}
                className="w-full py-3 px-4 rounded-md font-semibold transition-all hover:opacity-90 disabled:opacity-50"
                style={{ 
                  backgroundColor: nordColors.aurora[3],
                  color: nordColors.polarNight[0]
                }}
              >
                {loading ? 'Saving...' : 'Save User'}
              </button>
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Data Display Section */}
          <div 
            className="p-6 rounded-lg shadow-xl"
            style={{ backgroundColor: nordColors.polarNight[1] }}
          >
            <h2 
              className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-6 flex items-center gap-2"
              style={{ color: nordColors.frost[1] }}
            >
              <Database className="w-6 h-6" />
              User Database
            </h2>

            <button
              onClick={fetchUsers}
              disabled={loading}
              className="mb-6 py-2 px-4 rounded-md font-semibold transition-all hover:opacity-90 disabled:opacity-50"
              style={{ 
                backgroundColor: nordColors.frost[2],
                color: nordColors.snowStorm[2]
              }}
            >
              {loading ? 'Loading...' : 'Show All Users'}
            </button>

            {showTable && (
              <div className="overflow-x-auto">
                {users.length === 0 ? (
                  <p style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[0] }}>
                    No users found. Add some users using the form.
                  </p>
                ) : (
                  <table className="w-full">
                    <thead>
                      <tr style={{ borderBottom: `2px solid ${nordColors.polarNight[3]}` }}>
                        <th 
                          className="text-left py-3 px-2 font-semibold"
                          style={{ color: nordColors.frost[1] }}
                        >
                          Name
                        </th>
                        <th 
                          className="text-left py-3 px-2 font-semibold"
                          style={{ color: nordColors.frost[1] }}
                        >
                          Email
                        </th>
                        <th 
                          className="text-left py-3 px-2 font-semibold"
                          style={{ color: nordColors.frost[1] }}
                        >
                          Company
                        </th>
                      </tr>
                    </thead>
                    <tbody>
                      {users.map((user, index) => (
                        <tr 
                          key={user.ID}
                          className="hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity"
                          style={{ 
                            borderBottom: `1px solid ${nordColors.polarNight[2]}`,
                            backgroundColor: index % 2 === 0 ? nordColors.polarNight[0] : 'transparent'
                          }}
                        >
                          <td 
                            className="py-3 px-2"
                            style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[1] }}
                          >
                            {user.name}
                          </td>
                          <td 
                            className="py-3 px-2"
                            style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[1] }}
                          >
                            {user.email}
                          </td>
                          <td 
                            className="py-3 px-2"
                            style={{ color: nordColors.snowStorm[1] }}
                          >
                            {user.company}
                          </td>
                        </tr>
                      ))}
                    </tbody>
                  </table>
                )}
              </div>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

export default App;

 

App.css

 

@import "tailwindcss";

 

The Landing Page

 

Click on show all Users to see all data

Insert data in Form and Save User data

Data added

Conclusion

Our journey in this blog proved that React+Vite and SAP CAP can work together beautifully – but success requires understanding the nuances of integration, security, and deployment in the BTP environment,

My approach was to integrate with external APIs and services beyond the SAP ecosystem. For purely internal, form-heavy applications, UI5 might still be the more better choice.

Please feel free to ask any questions you may have. 

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model SAP Business Application Studio SAP Community
